stone_id.Karigar: All blank — Karigar sells melee goods parcels, not individual certified stones.

cert_number.Karigar: All blank — Karigar sells melee goods parcels, not individual certified stones.

lab.Karigar: All blank — Karigar sells melee goods parcels, not individual certified stones.

price_per_carat.Vaibhav: Column named "Amount" but contains price PER CARAT — not total price. All other suppliers use "Amount" for total price.
price_per_carat.Karigar: "Vendor Price" = cost price Karigar pays its own vendor — not the same as other suppliers' ask price.

price.Vaibhav: Column named "Total Amount" for total price. All other suppliers use "Amount".
price.Karigar: "Karigar" column = Karigar's marked-up sell price per carat — not a total parcel price.

length.Vaibhav: "Lenth" is a typo in source data — normalize to canonical length on load.

measurements.Ratnakala: No combined measurements column. Concatenate Length + Width + Depth into one string at text-build time.

depth_pct.Zhaveri: "Depth" = depth percentage (depth_pct), NOT raw mm depth. Ratnakala/Vaibhav "Depth" = raw mm.

pav_angle.Zhaveri: "Pa" = pav_angle. All other suppliers use "Pav Angle".

pav_depth.Zhaveri: "Pd" = pav_depth. All other suppliers use "Pav Height".

carat.Karigar: "Carat Weight" = average weight per stone in a parcel size range — not a single certified stone weight. Do not use for stone-level comparisons.

color.Karigar: "Color (IGI)" is a grade RANGE (e.g. G-H) — not a single GIA grade. Do not use for stone-level comparisons.

clarity.Karigar: "Purity (IGI)" is a grade RANGE (e.g. VS-SI) — not a single GIA grade. Do not use for stone-level comparisons.

collision_fields.price_per_carat_vs_price: Both price_per_carat and price share the source name "Amount" (Ratnakala, Glowstar, Zhaveri use it for total price; Vaibhav uses it for per-carat price). The script resolves this via supplier-aware tie-breaking: if a supplier is named, the first canonical field whose alias list contains the column AND is consistent with that supplier wins.
collision_fields.depth_vs_depth_pct: Ratnakala and Vaibhav use "Depth" for raw mm depth (canonical: depth). Zhaveri uses "Depth" for depth percentage (canonical: depth_pct). Without a supplier hint the script maps to depth_pct and warns.
collision_fields.resolution_strategy: When two canonical fields share a source alias, pass --supplier on the CLI. The script will prefer the canonical field that makes sense for that supplier.
